verdict_reasoning: SRC is a cytoplasmic non-receptor tyrosine kinase canonically localized to the inner leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation and palmitoylation, anchoring it to the cytoplasmic face. However, SRC has documented surface-facing pools in multiple contexts: (1) cancer cells display ecto-SRC activity and surface-exposed kinase domains as part of aberrant oncogenic signaling; (2) activated immune cells and platelets show regulated translocation of SRC to the outer leaflet during degranulation and activation; (3) SRC partners with integrins and growth-factor receptors in signaling complexes that position it near the membrane-proximal cytoplasmic face, enabling recruitment of downstream effectors. The canonical inner-leaflet anchor is unambiguous, but the documented non-baseline surface pool in cancer and activated immune cells, plus the juxtamembrane signaling-complex biology, qualifies SRC as contextual rather than purely cytoplasmic.
